modules.summary: Provides the authentication interface with username and password fields, options for guest access and cookie information, and inline error handling for failed sign-in attempts.
modules.where_it_fits: Acts as the application's primary entry point for unauthenticated users before access to Dashboard, My Courses, and course pages; a successful teacher login redirects to the Dashboard referenced in the top navigation, while guest access permits limited unauthenticated browsing of site content.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is unauthenticated and at the site's login page (or choosing guest access); valid credentials are available for successful sign-in.

modules.summary: Displays a personalized greeting and side-by-side Timeline and Calendar blocks summarizing upcoming teaching actions and month-view events.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the top navigation bar via the Dashboard link (alongside Home and My Courses), this module serves as the user’s personal overview/landing area after login and links into the dedicated calendar view and calendar data management pages. It sits before course-specific pages in the user flow, providing aggregated cross-course information that complements the Course Page and Course Index sidebar.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated (logged-in) user; user account exists and may have one or more enrolled courses (Timeline will be empty if none).

modules.summary: Provides an interface for customizing the Dashboard layout by adding, configuring, moving, and removing blocks, with an option to reset the page to the default layout.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the top navigation via the Dashboard link, this mode is the Dashboard-specific editing state where block management controls (add block, move, configure, delete) and layout persistence are exposed; it complements the regular Dashboard viewing path and sits alongside other course/activity edit modes in the app's navigation structure.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is authenticated and viewing the Dashboard page, with Dashboard edit capability enabled (Edit mode toggled on).

modules.summary: Displays all courses the user (typically a teacher) has access to as configurable visual cards with filtering, searching, sorting, and per-card actions.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the top navigation bar via the My Courses link (alongside Home and Dashboard), this page serves as the course-selection hub prior to entering a specific Course Page; selecting a course card navigates to that course's main page where the course tab bar and Course Index appear.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in user with access to one or more courses (e.g., a teacher)

modules.summary: Displays the full course content organized into collapsible sections with activities and resources listed under each section.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the main navigation via Home / Dashboard / My Courses or by selecting a course link (breadcrumbs reflect the path), this view is the central course workspace where the horizontal course tab bar and Course Index sidebar provide navigation between course-level screens and activities.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and has access to the course (enrolled student or teacher) with the course available in My Courses or Dashboard.

modules.module_title: Course Edit Mode and Activity Chooser
modules.summary: Enables an authoring interface on a Course page with inline controls for editing, managing sections and activities, bulk actions, and an Activity Chooser modal for adding activities and resources.
modules.where_it_fits: This module is activated on a Course Page (reached via My Courses or Dashboard → select course) and switches the course view into edit mode for content creation and organization; it is primarily used by teachers during course setup and ongoing content edits (the Settings tab is visible to teachers).
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in as a user with editing/teacher permissions and currently viewing an existing Course Page.

modules.summary: Form for configuring and creating an Assignment activity, including settings for availability, submission and feedback types, grading, completion, access restrictions, and notifications.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the Course Edit Mode via the Activity Chooser when managing a course (reachable from My Courses or a Course Page); after saving it returns to the course page or opens the new assignment's page. It complements course-level navigation such as the Course tab and Course Index sidebar for adding activities to a course.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User with course editing permissions viewing a specific course with Course Edit Mode enabled and the Activity Chooser open.

modules.summary: A collapsible form for viewing and editing a course's metadata, appearance, enrollment and participation settings, and completion/grouping options with inline validation and image/summary fields.
modules.where_it_fits: This module is accessed from a course's horizontal tab bar under the Settings tab (visible to teachers) and sits within the course page workflow between Course Page and Course Edit Mode, persisting changes back to the course view (Save and display) or aborting (Cancel). It is reachable via the course breadcrumbs and the Course Index when a specific course is active.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: An active specific course is open and the user has teacher/manager permissions such that the Settings tab is visible.

modules.summary: Lists and filters users enrolled in a course and provides per-user actions (view profile, edit role, message), bulk actions, and an enrolment dialog to add users with roles and durations.
modules.where_it_fits: Opened from a course's horizontal tab bar via the Participants tab (reachable from Home > My Courses > [Course] > Participants), it sits alongside Course, Grades and Activities and is used during course administration to manage enrolled users. The module complements course navigation (breadcrumbs and Course Index sidebar) by focusing on user-level management within a specific course.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and viewing a specific course, with permission to view and manage participants (including enrolling users) when enrollment actions are required.

modules.summary: Shows an individual assignment's metadata, grading summary metrics, description and attachments, and provides access to the per-student grading interface and assignment-related tabs.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessed from a course’s activity view (breadcrumbs and the course tab bar: Course / Activities) when viewing a specific assignment within a Course Page; it complements the Course Index sidebar and sits alongside tabs for Settings, Submissions, and Advanced grading in the assignment lifecycle.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in as a teacher and viewing a specific assignment activity within an existing course

modules.summary: Displays a searchable and filterable table of all student submission records for an assignment, including submission and grading statuses, timestamps, previews, feedback, and quick-grading inline controls.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from an assignment's activity page within a course (reachable via the Course tab and course breadcrumbs or the Course Index sidebar), this view is part of the teacher/grader workflow for reviewing and grading student work after submissions have been made. It sits downstream of Assignment Creation and is used prior to or during updating the Gradebook.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is viewing a specific course assignment and has grading permissions (e.g., teacher or grader), and at least the assignment exists (with one or more student submissions available).

modules.summary: A spreadsheet-style Grader report that displays enrolled students as rows and course activities as columns, with per-column and per-cell controls, inline editing when Edit mode is enabled, and class averages shown.
modules.where_it_fits: Located within a course's Grades tab in the horizontal course tab bar (Teachers see the Settings and Grades tabs), and selectable from the report-type dropdown when viewing grade reports; it complements other reports reachable from the user menu's Reports link and the course navigation. It is used during course assessment and grading workflows to view and edit grades for enrolled students.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in as a Teacher (role with grading privileges) viewing a specific course that has enrolled students and course activities, on the Grades tab with the Grader report available.

modules.summary: Displays the signed-in teacher's personal information, contact controls, activity links, privacy details, and login activity in organized information cards.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the top navigation bar user menu (Profile) available on every page, this module provides the user's personal hub alongside other user options like Grades and Preferences and is part of the account-management portion of the application's lifecycle after authentication.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in as a teacher with an active session

modules.summary: Allows a logged-in user to modify their personal information, avatar, optional names, interests, and site-defined custom fields via a validated, collapsible form with upload support.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the top navigation user menu (User initials → Profile → Edit) and part of account management within the global navigation; it is used after profile viewing and before returning to the refreshed profile page or other user-related areas like Private Files or Preferences. The module is independent of course-level navigation and appears in the global user settings flow reached from the header.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is authenticated (logged in) and has an existing profile record; the user has navigated to their Profile Edit screen via the top-right user menu.

modules.summary: Terminates the current authenticated session and redirects the user to the login page.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the user menu in the top navigation bar (right-side user initials dropdown) and appears in the global navigation; it is the final step in the session lifecycle that returns the application to the unauthenticated login state. After logout, access to protected pages (Home, Dashboard, My Courses, Course pages) requires re-authentication.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is currently authenticated (logged in) with an active session and the user menu is available.
